evals.prompt: Design the solution for spec 003 which has a requirements.md describing a payment processing system with Stripe integration.
evals.expected_output: A complete solution.md with architecture, components, interfaces, ADRs, and acceptance criteria
expectations: Read requirements.md before designing
Explored the existing codebase for patterns and conventions
Invoked brainstorm to evaluate technical approaches before committing
Wrote output to solution.md in the spec directory
Followed template.md structure exactly — all sections present
No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain in completed document
Architecture pattern is clearly stated with rationale
Components are MECE — each has a single distinct responsibility
Every PRD requirement maps to exactly one component
No two components own the same domain logic
All interfaces specified — endpoints, data models, contracts
All ADRs presented to user for confirmation before proceeding
Every ADR has rationale and trade-offs documented
Acceptance criteria use EARS format (UBIQUITOUS, EVENT-DRIVEN, STATE-DRIVEN, etc.)
Every PRD acceptance criterion has a corresponding EARS criterion
Document contains NO implementation code
Launched parallel specialist agents for research (architecture, security, performance)

evals.prompt: Design a solution, but the user rejects the first proposed architecture (microservices) and wants a monolith instead.
evals.expected_output: Skill adapts to user's architecture choice without pushing the rejected approach
expectations: Presented architecture options during brainstorm phase
Recorded user's rejection of microservices as an ADR
Redesigned components for monolith architecture
Did NOT continue with microservices after user rejected it
ADR documents the trade-offs of choosing monolith over microservices

evals.prompt: Design a solution for a feature that requires no database changes, no new APIs, and no external integrations — just internal refactoring of business logic.
evals.expected_output: SDD adapts scope appropriately — omits irrelevant sections rather than filling them with N/A
expectations: Database schema section is minimal or marked as no change
External interfaces section reflects no new integrations
Component design focuses on internal restructuring
Solution does NOT invent unnecessary infrastructure
Design stays within PRD scope — no scope creep

evals.prompt: Design a solution where two proposed components have overlapping responsibilities (both handle user validation).
evals.expected_output: MECE validation catches the overlap and resolves it
expectations: Ran MECE check on components
Detected that two components overlap in responsibility
Resolved the overlap by merging or re-partitioning
Final design has no two components owning the same domain logic
Documented the MECE resolution in the design

evals.prompt: Continue solution design for spec 002 which has a partially completed solution.md with 2 pending ADRs.
evals.expected_output: Resume from existing state, present pending ADRs for confirmation
expectations: Read existing solution.md from the spec directory
Identified the 2 pending ADRs
Presented each pending ADR to user for confirmation
Did NOT rewrite sections that were already complete
Updated ADR status from Pending to Confirmed after user approval
